entries.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
entries.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

entries.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
entries.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

entries.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
entries.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

entries.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
entries.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

entries.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

entries.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
entries.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

entries.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
entries.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

entries.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
entries.description: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.

entries.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
entries.description: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

entries.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
entries.description: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

entries.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
entries.description: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

entries.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
entries.description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied

entries.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
entries.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

entries.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
entries.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

entries.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
entries.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

entries.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
entries.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

entries.title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
entries.description: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” t

entries.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales

entries.title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
entries.description: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.

entries.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups
entries.description: Station F, a Paris-based startup hub founded by French billionaire Xavier Niel, is gearing up for a new edition of its F/ai accelerator program in a bid to strengthen its positioning as a stepping sto

news.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
news.description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.

news.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
news.description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

news.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
news.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

news.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
news.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

news.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
news.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

news.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
news.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

news.title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
news.description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

news.title: Amazon will stop accepting new customers for Mechanical Turk
news.description: These may be the last days of Amazon’s Mechanical Turk.

news.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
news.description: Two hundred and fifty years after the signing of the Declaration of Independence, a new commercial asks: What if the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace?

news.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
news.description: As part of an ongoing legal dispute with three Hollywood studios, Midjourney is seeking to compel those studios to reveal how they use AI themselves.

news.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
news.description: Alibaba has reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk software.

news.title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human

news.title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
news.description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.

news.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
news.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

news.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
news.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

news.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
news.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.
